user_query:
I’d like your help turning my March farm logs into a simple monthly update my sister Evelyn can share with her friends and partners abroad.

Inputs are in the workspace:
- Harvest log: input/harvest_log.csv
- Expenses: input/expenses.csv
- Farm details and targets: config/farm.yaml
- Report settings (period, version, units): config/report_config.py

Please:
1) Read REPORT_PERIOD, REPORT_VERSION, and UNIT_LABEL from config/report_config.py. Use REPORT_PERIOD to filter both CSVs (include only records whose date falls within that YYYY-MM). Use UNIT_LABEL for all harvest units in outputs. Read farm_name, location, sibling_name, audience_label, currency, and per-crop targets from config/farm.yaml.
2) Compute period statistics from the filtered records:
   - Total harvest across all crops (in UNIT_LABEL), rounded to one decimal.
   - Number of unique harvest days in the period.
   - Average harvest per harvest day across all crops (total divided by the number of unique harvest days), rounded to one decimal.
   - Per-crop totals (sum of quantity_kg for each crop in the period), the number of harvest days each crop appears on (unique dates where that crop has entries), and the average per harvest day for that crop (rounded to one decimal).
   - Total expenses for the period (in currency from config), and a breakdown by category, each rounded to two decimals. Identify the single highest expense category by total.
   - Compare each crop’s period total to its target from config/farm.yaml. Compute diff_kg = total_kg − target_kg and whether the target was met (total_kg ≥ target_kg).
3) Write a concise Markdown report to output/monthly_update.md with these sections:
   - Title line: "<farm_name> Monthly Update — <Month YYYY> (v<REPORT_VERSION>)" where <Month YYYY> is the month name and year derived from REPORT_PERIOD.
   - Period and Source: include the exact REPORT_PERIOD string, the number of harvest rows included from input/harvest_log.csv after filtering, and the number of expense rows included from input/expenses.csv after filtering.
   - Key Figures: a short bullet list with total harvest, number of harvest days, average harvest per harvest day, total expenses (with currency symbol/name), and a sorted (descending by total_kg) bullet list of per-crop lines in the form "Crop: X.X UNIT_LABEL (avg per harvest day: Y.Y UNIT_LABEL; days: D)".
   - Targets Check: a short line per crop present in the period showing target_kg, total_kg, diff_kg (with sign), and "Met target" or "Short of target".
   - Narrative: 4–6 sentences, down-to-earth and clear, addressed to "<sibling_name> and <audience_label>", that references the computed numbers. Mention the crop(s) that met or exceeded target, note at least one area needing attention (a crop below target), and name the highest expense category. Please keep it friendly without inventing facts beyond the data.
4) Also write a machine-readable per-crop summary to output/crop_summary.csv with header:
   crop,total_kg,harvest_days,avg_per_harvest_day_kg,target_kg,diff_kg,met_target
   - Include one row per crop present in the period.
   - Round all kg values to one decimal and keep met_target as true/false.
   - Sort rows by total_kg descending.

Notes and constraints:
- Ignore any records outside REPORT_PERIOD.
- Do not hardcode the month or version; pull them from config/report_config.py.
- Use the units and currency symbols/names from the config files.
- This should be fully reproducible from the provided files, and both outputs must be created under the output/ directory. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
